title: alcea dissecta
intro: Alcea dissecta, also known as the cut-leaved hollyhock or dissected hollyhock, is a perennial flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Alcea dissecta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
